summary: Loader-stub package silently pulls unrelated scoped dependency on require()
details: The package is published as @sql-access/nodesql but its index.js is a verbatim copy of the feross/buffer polyfill, and the README is titled 'bare-stream' — three unrelated identities stitched together. As the very first executable statement, index.js:11 contains `import('@sqlite-node/createsql');`, which has no relationship to Buffer/stream functionality. Any consumer that `require()`s this package automatically loads the @sqlite-node/createsql dependency at runtime; the Buffer polyfill body is cover for hosting that side-effecting import. package.json declares `"@sqlite-node/createsql": "^1.0.6"` and an empty description. The mismatched name/README/code combined with a top-level dynamic import of an unrelated scoped package is the loader-stub + namespace-abuse pattern: an installer searching for a SQL helper or Buffer polyfill unknowingly pulls an unrelated attacker-staged scoped package into their dependency tree.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
